wrongClaims.ourClaim: Jazz fusion is a popular music genre that developed in the late 1960s when musicians combined jazz harmony and improvisation with rock music, funk, and rhythm and blues.
wrongClaims.problem: The entire albumDNA is the Wikipedia 'Jazz fusion' genre-article intro copied verbatim; it describes a music genre, not this album (a 2019 smooth-jazz compilation by The Jazz Messengers / Piano Dreamers). It is content about a different kind of thing.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: Jazz fusion (also known as jazz rock, jazz-rock fusion, or simply fusion) is a popular music genre that developed in the late 1960s when musicians combined jazz harmony and improvisation with rock music, funk, and rhythm and blues.

notes: The MB release itself is correctly resolved (embedded_mbid: title matches, artists The Jazz Messengers + Piano Dreamers, label Diamond Music Record, 2019-09-21) and all five keyTracks match the MB tracklist — so the catalog row points at the right album. The problem is the prose + wikipediaUrl: both belong to the Jazz_fusion genre page, not this album. Classic disambiguation-collision disease, here onto a genre article. Repair: replace the genre boilerplate with album-specific prose. wikiLinkVerdict wrong_entity (stored_url is en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jazz_fusion).
